long_description: At [2024-12-07 03:00:00] UTC IBM Cloud engineers have been identified via the customer case  for NetApp issue. Customers attempting  to delete, stop or start VSIs  may experience hung state residing their VM,s on those impacted nodes in mzone737.
close_notes:
Service team migrated the impacted VSI  by lif migration to resolve the errors

Service Protection Plan
==================
The status will be monitored by running a scrip every 30 minutes, until further notice, to ensure no further issues develop
Root cause is being investigated by an extended team of compute, network and storage
current_status_next_steps:
All affected VSIs have been resolved (total of 19)
Team are monitoring for a prolonged period (at least 24 hours) to ensure there are no further issues
Investigation is ongoing for a potential link between the VSI mounting failures and a compute upgrade in DAL.

 -- runbook link --
customer_facing_impact:
Users attempting to delete, stop or start their VSIs may experience operations entering a hung state.
New provisioning doesn't seem to be affected.
chronology:
Timeline copied from INC10494336

Response Team Participants (add abbrev. role and name as needed)
=====================
SRE(s): Gopi Selvaraj(IaaS CORE), Amul Sharma, Phanindra , Sangamesh Neelannavar , Satyam Singh. Aparna Srinivasan
STSM: Shajeer Mohammed
NET: Manish Kumar
MGR: Ankit Rastogi (IaaS-CORE), John Diez-Daly, 
iPOPs:ANDRE GONZAGA, Ricardo Martins
AVM: Shruti A, George Nazarov
Storage: Arun Gopinathan, Harkirat Singh, Arun Gopinathan, Jagannath Thummala
VP: Girish Dhanakshirur, RAGHUNATHAN KRISHNAMURTHY
Storage Dir: Devarajan Karalil, Joe Loas
Storage vendor Profe: Joseph Caruso
Compute: K Vinay Kumar
IAAS Dir: Jason Todoroff

Slack(s):  #  #warroom-vpc-inc10494336
https://ibm.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A217Q2P18
MS Teams:  https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NTQ4OTQ1MWQtM2I4YS00ZTI3LTk1ZjUtYmUzNTNkNmUxMDNj%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22fcf67057-50c9-4ad4-98f3-ffca64add9e9%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%2202e8d573-9183-45d5-84d2-0959608be478%22%7d

Times in UTC
[2025-Dec-07]

03:35 Sev 3 CIE Confirmed
03:36 Shruti Joining as AVM OnDuty
03:40 CIE has been raised by identifying the issue via case : https://github.ibm.com/cloudlab/ipops-vpc-cases/issues/7463
03:41 AVM has requested the required details
03:50 Team still needs to validate the exact outage start, will confirm in few mins.
03:50 Core team has paged netapp team PDE7713214
03:57 Storage has joined the call and team is investigating
04:04 Storage team is working to perform the Lif migration to mitigate the issue
04:18 team is checking if the NFS server is responding for other working compute  node: working
04:19 team finds the issue is intermittent.
04:33 Team performed a LIF migration (for 51d node to node c) , which went successful
04:33 team is checking further for other nodes where nfs is hung
04:35 1st lif IP (10.200.143.135) was migrated at 4.29 AM UTC
04:40 Core is providing latest status of not responding IPS to storage support
05:16 Team validated there are no DNS issues linked to this.
05:25 Deva, it looks like there's a data path impact on customers. So if you're seeing so many NFS server that mean those Q trees may be hanging. And all those customer volumes may may be experiencing the problem already. So we need, you know whatever it takes to troubleshoot this further. we also need to check on the host OS
05:34 AVM asks to gather the list of customers on the affected nodes( since all 3 clusters are impacted)
05:35 All the lifs are now migrated affecting the user case reported
05:35 Team is trying to get Neteng and Host os team
05:36 ACS to check with customer if their VSI is fine post the lif migration performed
05:44 Host OS has joined , there was base OS update that happened recently on dial and across the other M zones. This is .34 version of base OS and we are suspecting that something in that has affected. Trying to check what must have broken the NFS
06:08 Team decided to ask the customer to stop and restart the BSA/VSA (real state showing "starting"), and verify NFS server errors for three IPs.
06:21 Network ACS: received a response from the customer—after stopping the VSI and attempting to start it again, it got stuck once more.
06:21 IPops informed VSI is in running state. Asked to reverify with customer
06:29 NetEng team also to be engaged.
06:40 : Network connectivity and services appear fine across all nodes (RPC info, IP routes, interface activity), but the issue is limited to mounting specific hosts. Initial analysis on four nodes confirms network and routing are okay; only the mount operation is failing.
06:50 Storage SRE: The issue is not related to a simple stop/start scenario. Currently, 10 compute nodes are reporting NFS server not responding, with 8 nodes on RK 4/21 and others on RK 366 and 363. Blast radius appears limited to 17 VSIs, but if it's a shared flex volume, the impact could extend beyond these instances.
07:17 Customer cases are resolved. CIE to continue as the issue is fully fixed/root cause is not identified.
7:20 Neteng team reconfimred that path including every physical interface as well and there is no issue in the network.
7:45 Manual mount and unmount working on effected node.
8:15 Currently 17 VSI's which are effected and collected details of VSI.
09:20 Current plan: After the lif migration, 17 mount points had issues; 4 recovered. When we reverted the LIF, 4 failed again. So, plan is to keep those 4 stable and focus on the remaining 13 mount points that are still stuck. We need to identify which NetApp nodes are responsible and fix them. Instead of more LIF migrations, Storage recommend rebooting the nodes one at a time to recover the remaining mount points and ensure all VSIs are running.
09:30 Rebooting the compute nodes in progress. Script is being executed by Storage and ipops
10:14 SRE Node vsff-dal1351e rebooted. Node vsff-dal1351a reboot ongoing (delayed because of ASUP collection)
10:24 IPOPS 10 mount failures currently
11:02 Working on background to restore operation for the other 9 VSIs. focusing on 1351 node A
11:26 Now we are down to 8 mount failures
11:26 IPOPS There are intermittent problems in the network. Once a GT results in time-out, the second time was working properly.
11:28 Ran the status check script again. It shows 14 mount failures now. 
11:41 Raising to S2, based on executive request
12:10 Affected VSIs number reduced to 8
12:55 Affected VSIs number reduced to 6
13:30 Performing LIF migration for the remaining 5 affected VSIs
13:50 Investigation is ongoing for a potential link between the VSI mounting failures and a compute upgrade in DAL.
13:58 Affected VSIs number reduced to 2 (these are new, not part of the original 17)
14:30 99% of the hung errors are originating from compute nodes in the same rack. Current theory is that we have a network issue
15:06 Finall 2 VSIs have been migrated, running script to confirm we are all clear
15:24 IPOPS will be running the script every 30 minutes until further notice.

-----------------
NRE timeline:
-----------------
7 Dec 2025 05:05 UTC - NRE joined  warroom-vpc-inc10494336 and begun investigating NFS issues in Node dal3-qz1-sr1-rk421-s04
7 Dec 2025 06:06 UTC - INGIT19841 created
7 Dec 2025 06:22 UTC - Paged Net-eng: https://ibm.pagerduty.com/incidents/Q3OM836O9KOKUE
7 Dec 2025 15:33 UTC - NRE checked affected path for source/destination prefixes and route was present end to end
7 Dec 2025 07:52 UTC - Arista came on the call case 757770
8 Dec 2025 05:13 UTC - NRE team observing Stale entries on ngas02.sr01.dal13 for some of the impacted Sources.
8 Dec 2025 06:57 UTC - ngas02.sr01.dal13 has been costed out
8 Dec 2025 09:10 UTC - ngas02.sr01.dal13 has been reloaded
8 Dec 2025 09:37 UTC - ngas02.sr01.dal13 has been costed in
8 Dec 2025 09:53 UTC - Starting CHG14244810 | Reload of ngas01.sr01.dal13
8 Dec 2025 10:22 UTC - Completed CHG14244810 | Reload of ngas01.sr01.dal13
8 Dec 2025 15:18 UTC - NRE Confirmed CIE INC10505174 created as retro CIE to be used as Parent


=============================
               FOR AVM REFERENCE
=============================

Issue is frequently repetitive. The logs will be shared with netapp to investigate , and to check if that can happen to other clusters.
